topic.what: Adaptive Defense Orchestration framework for RAG systems against membership inference and data poisoning attacks
topic.how: Sentinel-Strategist architecture performing lightweight threat detection then selectively deploying defenses through pipeline hooks
topic.why_matters: Resolves security-utility paradox where always-on defenses cause 41-46% contextual recall degradation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Retrieval quality preservation under attack/defense conditions
characteristics.vs_baseline: Restored to >75% of undefended baseline under data poisoning
characteristics.evidence: Section 6 experimental results across benchmark datasets
characteristics.context: ADO variants vs always-on static stack vs undefended baseline

characteristics.evidence: Section 6 evaluated across three benchmark datasets
characteristics.context: Under MIA threat with ADO active defense selection

characteristics.what_it_measures: Utility cost from static full-defense stack
characteristics.vs_baseline: Always-on defense stack vs undefended baseline
characteristics.evidence: Experiments across multiple datasets confirm retrieval as primary bottleneck
characteristics.context: Static stack causes retrieval phase collapse; LLM generator remains unimpaired

characteristics.what_it_measures: Defense robustness variation across LLM choices
characteristics.vs_baseline: Performance varies significantly with model selection
characteristics.evidence: Paper notes robustness remains sensitive to model choice
characteristics.context: Five orchestration models evaluated; content leakage not empirically validated

novelty: Sentinel-Strategist architecture decoupling threat assessment from defense selection
Empirical quantification of security-utility paradox in RAG
Adaptive query-aware defense selection vs static always-on approaches
Unified orchestration across membership inference and data poisoning vectors
open_problems: Content leakage defense (AV-filter) not empirically validated; architectural only
Joint impact of concurrent multi-vector threats not fully explored
Defense registry evolution and long-term calibration needs study
